experiment: IMP4 — do conditional conformal methods clear the absolute high-synergy coverage bar?
HONEST_VERDICT: No conditional method DOMINATES the scalar SCC prior. Mondrian (per-tercile quantile) improves high-synergy coverage on CaRPool but DEGRADES marginal coverage on Norman (0.87<0.88). Conditional-quantile is no better than vanilla. SCC satisfies the pre-registered bar (high-synergy CI contains 0.90 AND marginal>=0.88) on BOTH datasets, but its POINT coverage stays below nominal (0.82 Norman / 0.86 CaRPool) — the CI includes 0.90 largely because n~130-140 is small. So the honest reading is 'statistically consistent with nominal', NOT 'demonstrably achieves nominal'.
what_this_ADDS: Rigor: we pre-registered and tested 3 alternative conditional-conformal methods against the current scalar SCC. None beats it cleanly. This strengthens the claim that the SCC scalar prior is a reasonable choice, and it honestly confirms the absolute-coverage bar is HARD at this sample size — not a framing artifact.
what_this_does_NOT_claim: Does NOT claim the absolute coverage problem is solved. Does NOT change the merit tier (78). The point estimate remains below nominal on both datasets; a demonstrable clear would need either more data (tighter CI) or a genuinely better conditional method, neither found here.
no_free_lunch: Widths: conditional methods clear (where they do) partly by adjusting width; none achieves nominal point coverage at lower width without cost.
